PETITION RE SUBSTITUTED JUDGMENT TRUST
Off Calendar Mar-11-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
Jennifer Steneberg for petitioner, Fe Soledad Samot.

The Court noted that it is not inclined to approve the fees over 1%, that the request for $500.00 allowance seems very unusual for a special needs trust and that there is no supporting information on what it will go for. Ms. Steneberg stated the $500.00 is not an allowance, that it was intended to give the court an estimate of the monthly expenses, and the same was intended as a broad estimate for the travel expense request. The Court noted they agree on the bond issue, and the last issue to be addressed is the reimbursement provision under Article 7 of the Trust instrument. Ms. Steneberg noted the agreement is inconsistent with the master trust agreement, referenced page 16 of revised joinder agreement as cited on paragraph 4 of her declaration. The court noted the attachment to the declaration is different than the attachment on the proposed order, that exhibit A to the Jan. 8th declaration does make it clear, that it needs the master pooled trust agreement and joinder agreement attachments to the proposed order submitted to the court by March 5th at noon.

The petition for order directing establishment of pooled special needs trust account, etc. is taken under submission. $71,000.00 bond is required.

Status on filing of bond set for March 30, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Status of first account is set for May 10, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
